tagline: Leading cloud-native identity and access management platform with SSO, MFA, and lifecycle management for the enterprise

D1.evidence: Okta is a leading IAM platform with native OAuth 2.0/OIDC support including PKCE flows. Token auto-renewal via refresh tokens with configurable lifetimes. No plaintext credential storage — all passwords hashed (bcrypt/argon2), API tokens encrypted at rest. MFA is a core feature with 30+ factors (Okta Verify, FIDO2/WebAuthn, YubiKey, Google Authenticator, SMS, email). Enterprise SSO via SAML 2.0 as both IdP and SP. API tokens support granular OAuth scopes (okta.users.read, okta.apps.manage, etc.). Deep AD/LDAP integration with delegated authentication and directory sync. Bearer tokens transmitted exclusively over TLS 1.2+. Okta for Government High holds FedRAMP High authorization.
missing: API key scope granularity could be more fine-grained at the endpoint level

D2.evidence: Okta implements multi-level RBAC with Super Admin, Org Admin, App Admin, Group Admin, Read-Only Admin, and custom admin roles with granular permissions. Read-only admin role available. File-level operation scoping not directly applicable (IAM platform). Default-deny access policies require explicit authorization. Dangerous operations (user deletion, policy changes) require admin-level permissions with audit logging. No sandbox concept as IAM platform — evaluated as N/A. Session timeout controls configurable per application. Admin policies centrally pushed to all users via admin console.
missing: No file operation scoping (not applicable to IAM)
No sandbox model (not applicable to IAM)
Default-deny at app level, not at individual operation level

D3.evidence: Okta supports HTTPS proxy for outbound connections. Proxy settings configurable for agent-based integrations. Custom API endpoints limited — Okta is cloud-native SaaS (okta.com / okta-emea.com). Enterprise CA certificate support for SAML/OIDC integrations. Okta collects system logs and usage analytics by default for service improvement (opt-out available for some). No full on-premises deployment — Okta Access Gateway provides hybrid AD bridge but core IAM is cloud. Privacy policy states customer data is not used to train AI/ML models. Data boundary documentation comprehensive with DPA, sub-processor lists, and transfer impact assessments.
missing: No fully on-premises deployment option (cloud-native SaaS)
Limited custom API endpoint configuration
Some telemetry/analytics collected by default

D4.evidence: Every authentication event, API call, and admin action is logged with full context (actor, target, action, timestamp, client IP, user agent). System Log API provides comprehensive programmable access to all audit events. Logs auto-persisted to Okta cloud with configurable retention (90 days standard, extended with SIEM). Sessions and audit trails exportable via API and CSV. Native SIEM integration via Syslog, Splunk, QRadar, Microsoft Sentinel, Sumo Logic connectors. Log immutability guaranteed by Okta's architecture — admins cannot modify system logs. Log retention configurable with extended storage options.
missing: Cryptographic log tamper-proofing (e.g., blockchain hashing) not explicitly documented
Extended log retention beyond 90 days requires SIEM integration or additional licensing

D5.evidence: Prompt injection defense is not applicable to Okta as an IAM platform — it is not an AI coding assistant or LLM-based tool. Okta does not process user prompts or execute AI-generated code. The platform uses machine learning for risk-based authentication (Okta Identity Intelligence) but this is not susceptible to prompt injection attacks in the traditional sense. All 8 checks are scored as N/A.
missing: Prompt injection defense is not in scope for an IAM platform
All 8 checks are N/A for this product category

D6.evidence: Okta Integration Network (OIN) provides 7000+ pre-built integrations with third-party applications. App integrations run via standard SAML/OIDC/SCIM protocols — not in isolated processes. Admin can whitelist/blacklist apps via app assignment policies. OAuth consent framework with admin-controlled scopes. Integrations inherit tenant's RBAC and access policies. Admins can restrict third-party app additions via admin console settings. API Access Management provides OAuth-based authorization for custom integrations.
missing: No isolated process model for integrations (all run within Okta's SaaS)
No signed configuration protection for OIN integrations explicitly documented

D7.evidence: Okta admin console provides centralized policy management with group-level, app-level, and org-level configurations. Admin policies cannot be overridden by end users — policy precedence is enforced. Policy inheritance supports priority ordering (highest priority wins). Admin console pushes all policies in real-time. Configuration change tracking with full audit trail. Environment variable override not applicable (SaaS platform). Universal Directory supports hierarchical group structures with policy cascading.
missing: No environment variable override mechanism (SaaS platform)
Configuration version history/rollback limited compared to git-based systems

D8.evidence: Okta provides real-time user activity dashboards showing authentication events, MFA challenges, and failed logins. Cost estimation per-user/per-feature based on subscription tier. No auto-compression concept applicable to IAM. Budget controls limited to license count management (user-based licensing). Anomaly detection via Okta Identity Intelligence (risk scoring, impossible travel). Per-application usage reporting available for admins.
missing: No real-time token/credit usage (not a token-based service)
No auto-compression mechanism (not applicable)
Limited budget hard-limit controls
Chargeback reporting is basic

D9.evidence: DPA available for all enterprise customers with GDPR Article 28 compliance. SOC 2 Type II certified, ISO 27001, ISO 27017, ISO 27018, FedRAMP High (Okta for Government). Data subject rights fully supported — deletion, access, correction, portability via admin API. Data residency options include US, EU (okta-emea.com), APAC regions. AI content labeling not applicable (not an AI content generation tool). Security incident notification process defined in DPA with 72-hour breach notification.
missing: AI content labeling not applicable (not an AI content tool)
Data residency granularity varies by plan tier

risks.title: Cloud-only architecture with no full on-premises option
risks.desc: Okta is a cloud-native SaaS platform. While Okta Access Gateway provides hybrid AD bridge capabilities, the core IAM service runs exclusively in Okta's cloud. Organizations with strict data sovereignty requirements requiring fully on-premises IAM cannot use Okta directly.

risks.title: Historical security incidents (2023 breach)
risks.desc: Okta experienced a significant security breach in 2023 involving unauthorized access to its support case management system. While the breach was contained and remediated, it raised concerns about the concentration of identity management in a single cloud provider.

risks.title: Telemetry and analytics data collection
risks.desc: Okta collects system logs, usage analytics, and performance telemetry by default. While privacy policy states data is not used for AI/ML training, the breadth of data collection may concern organizations with strict data handling requirements.

risks.title: Premium features locked behind higher licensing tiers
risks.desc: Critical security features like Identity Threat Protection, Identity Governance, and advanced SIEM integration require premium (P2/Workforce Identity) licensing, creating cost pressure for organizations that need comprehensive security coverage.

recs.text: For organizations with strict data sovereignty requirements, negotiate enhanced DPA terms specifying data residency within preferred regions. Implement Okta Access Gateway for hybrid AD scenarios to minimize cloud dependency for core authentication flows.

Configure Syslog/SIEM integration to export all Okta System Logs to an on-premises SIEM (Splunk, QRadar, Sentinel) for long-term retention and independent audit trail. Implement real-time alerting on critical admin actions and authentication anomalies.

Enforce phishing-resistant MFA (FIDO2/WebAuthn, Okta Verify with number challenge) as the default factor for all users. Implement Okta Identity Threat Protection with behavioral analysis and continuous risk-based authentication.

Implement strict third-party app governance: restrict OIN app installations to admin-approved apps only, require OAuth scope review for all custom integrations, and conduct quarterly reviews of active app assignments and their permissions.

Implement least-privilege admin access using custom admin roles. Enable Access Reviews for periodic certification of user permissions. Use Okta Governance for automated lifecycle management and deprovisioning.

vetoChecks.label: SOC 2 Type II certified with comprehensive compliance portfolio
End-to-end encryption (TLS 1.2+) for all data in transit and at rest
Data not used for AI/ML model training per privacy policy
FedRAMP High authorized for government workloads
